import heapq

def connect_ropes_min_cost(rope_lengths):
    """
    Connects n ropes with minimum cost.

    Args:
        rope_lengths: A list of integers representing the lengths of the ropes.

    Returns:
        An integer representing the minimum cost to connect all ropes.
    """
    if not rope_lengths:
        return 0

    # Create a min-heap from the rope lengths
    heapq.heapify(rope_lengths)

    total_cost = 0

    # Continue until only one rope remains in the heap
    while len(rope_lengths) > 1:
        # Extract the two smallest ropes
        first = heapq.heappop(rope_lengths)
        second = heapq.heappop(rope_lengths)

        # Cost to connect them is their sum
        current_cost = first + second
        total_cost += current_cost

        # Add the new combined rope back to the heap
        heapq.heappush(rope_lengths, current_cost)

    return total_cost
